expectations.evidence: All 5 test classes have '@group group_ai_pm' in the class-level PHPDoc: EntityCrudTest.php line 11, AccessControlTest.php line 12, CronQueueTest.php line 11, SettingsFormFunctionalTest.php line 10, DashboardFunctionalTest.php line 10

EntityCrudTest.php line 13: 'class EntityCrudTest extends KernelTestBase'; AccessControlTest.php line 14: 'class AccessControlTest extends KernelTestBase'. Both entity CRUD and access control tests correctly use KernelTestBase, not BrowserTestBase.
All three kernel tests include 'system', 'user', and 'group_ai_pm' in protected static $modules. EntityCrudTest.php lines 20-28, AccessControlTest.php lines 21-29, CronQueueTest.php lines 20-28.

All three kernel test setUp() methods call both $this->installEntitySchema('project') and $this->installEntitySchema('task'). EntityCrudTest.php lines 36-37, AccessControlTest.php lines 37-38, CronQueueTest.php lines 36-37.
All three kernel test setUp() methods call $this->installEntitySchema('user'). EntityCrudTest.php line 35, AccessControlTest.php line 36, CronQueueTest.php line 35.
All three kernel test setUp() methods call $this->installConfig(['group_ai_pm']). EntityCrudTest.php line 38, AccessControlTest.php line 39, CronQueueTest.php line 38.

Both BrowserTestBase subclasses declare 'protected $defaultTheme = "starterkit_theme"' rather than 'stark' or 'claro'. SettingsFormFunctionalTest.php line 30, DashboardFunctionalTest.php line 30. The expectation explicitly requires 'stark' or 'claro'.

All kernel test setUp() methods call parent::setUp() as the first statement: EntityCrudTest.php line 34, AccessControlTest.php line 35, CronQueueTest.php line 34. The two functional test classes (SettingsFormFunctionalTest, DashboardFunctionalTest) define no setUp() method, so there is no violation.

Kernel tests reside at tests/src/Kernel/ (EntityCrudTest.php, AccessControlTest.php, CronQueueTest.php) and functional tests reside at tests/src/Functional/ (SettingsFormFunctionalTest.php, DashboardFunctionalTest.php). Correct directory layout confirmed by file listing.

Kernel namespaces: 'Drupal\Tests\group_ai_pm\Kernel' (EntityCrudTest.php line 3, AccessControlTest.php line 3, CronQueueTest.php line 3). Functional namespaces: 'Drupal\Tests\group_ai_pm\Functional' (SettingsFormFunctionalTest.php line 3, DashboardFunctionalTest.php line 3). All match the required pattern.

SettingsFormFunctionalTest.php lines 44-45: '$user = $this->createUser(["administer group_ai_pm"]); $this->drupalLogin($user);' before accessing the settings page at line 47. Pattern matches exactly what the expectation requires.

SettingsFormFunctionalTest.php: statusCodeEquals(403) at line 37 (anonymous user), statusCodeEquals(200) at line 48 (authorized user), and statusCodeEquals(403) at line 184 (unauthorized logged-in user). DashboardFunctionalTest.php also has statusCodeEquals(403) at line 37 and statusCodeEquals(200) at line 48.
'datetime' is included in protected static $modules for all three kernel tests: EntityCrudTest.php line 23, AccessControlTest.php line 23, CronQueueTest.php line 23.

CronQueueTest.php calls 'group_ai_pm_cron()' directly (lines 66, 102, 136, 174, 219, 299, 333) rather than '$this->container->get("cron")->run()' or '\Drupal::service("cron")->run()' as required by the expectation. The module's cron hook is invoked directly, bypassing the cron service pipeline.

runtime_assertions.evidence: PASS — all 5 files have no syntax errors
PASS — PHPUnit discovers ~47 tests across all files (more comprehensive than WITHOUT variant)

FAIL — Kernel tests error: 'flexible_permissions.chain_calculator service not found' (Group module dependency missing from $modules). WITH variant included more modules than WITHOUT (datetime, views, field, group) showing better dependency awareness, but missed the flexible_permissions transitive dependency.
NOT RUN — depends on rt-5 passing, which failed
